# Tyler Rongxuan Chen

> Ph.D. candidate in Political Science and Scientific Computing at the University of Michigan
> (M.A. in Statistics in progress). Comparative politics and political methodology. Studies
> population structure — the spatially arranged communities that parties rest on — and how it
> shapes party systems, with work on Malaysia, Taiwan, Japan, Indonesia, and the United States.
> On the 2026–27 academic job market.

## Research focus

- Political parties and party systems; ethnic and urban–rural cleavages; dominant-party breakdown.
- Political methodology: ecological inference (the BACH estimator), spatial models for shifting
  electoral boundaries, and corrections for measurement error in demographic panels.
- Regional depth in Malaysia, with extensions to Taiwan, Japan, Indonesia, and the United States
  (Latino re-sorting in Florida; the overseas-Chinese community in New York City).

## Pages

- [Home](https://tylerrongxuanchen.github.io/index.html): biography and research overview.
- [Research](https://tylerrongxuanchen.github.io/research.html): peer-reviewed publications, papers under review, and working papers, each with a short summary.
- [Data](https://tylerrongxuanchen.github.io/data.html): public datasets, including the Malaysia Election Panel (1959–2018) and three-country candidate career panels.
- [Teaching](https://tylerrongxuanchen.github.io/teaching.html): methods-sequence and comparative-politics teaching.
- [CV](https://tylerrongxuanchen.github.io/cv.html): full curriculum vitae (PDF).
